description_long: This object is an antique-style, single-barrel shotgun with a flintlock firing mechanism, featuring a curved wooden grip and a short, thick metal barrel. The wood of the handle is dark and appears worn, with visible grain and some areas of discoloration, suggesting age and use. The metal components, including the barrel, trigger, and lock mechanism, have a matte, slightly weathered finish, with some areas showing a patina or rust. The trigger guard is rounded and relatively small, closely hugging the grip, and there is a metal ring at the base of the handle, likely for attaching a lanyard. The overall design is compact, with no visible stock beyond the pistol-style grip, and the barrel is unadorned, lacking ribbing or venting. This type of weapon would be more commonly seen as a historical artifact or in period reenactments rather than in modern urban environments.
description: A compact, antique flintlock shotgun with a curved wooden grip and a short, thick metal barrel, featuring a rounded trigger guard and aged finishes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the shotgun, highlighting the curved wooden grip, flintlock mechanism, and short barrel.
description_view_1: Front view down the barrel, emphasizing the circular muzzle and the alignment of the barrel and lock mechanism.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the other side of the grip, lock, and barrel, with similar wear and detailing as the first side.
description_view_3: Rear view from the grip, showing the rounded end of the handle and the alignment of the barrel and trigger guard.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, short barrel
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, small, closely fitted to the grip, no finger grooves or reinforcement
